docs: update beads issue tracking for handoff-contract enhancement

Record implementation notes and validation evidence for
ft-3681t.3.7.1 (handoff-contract deconfliction enhancement),
documenting specific helper functions, extended tests, and
validation status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-3681t.3.7.1","title":"[swarm][handoff] Explicit auto-resolve handoff contract in mission deconfliction messages","description":"Add explicit safe-handoff contract metadata and human-readable guidance to mission deconfliction messages when conflicts are auto-resolved, so losing agents have deterministic next actions. Scope: crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs + targeted tests for message content and thread routing. Validation: focused tests + rch-offloaded cargo check/test evidence.","notes":"Implemented scoped handoff-contract enhancement in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs` for child bead `ft-3681t.3.7.1`.

Code changes:
- `generate_conflict_messages` now emits recipient-specific handoff guidance instead of one shared body.
- Added explicit handoff contract block in each message body with deterministic fields:
  - role (`winner` / `loser` / `participant`)
  - action (`retain_assignment` / `yield_assignment` / `hold_and_retry` / `await_manual_resolution` / `observe_resolution`)
  - `thread_id` continuity target
  - continuity guidance sentence
- Added helper `handoff_contract_for_recipient(...)`.
- Extended tests:
  - `conflict_detection_auto_resolved_messages_include_handoff_roles`
  - `conflict_detection_manual_resolution_message_has_explicit_wait_action`
  - strengthened `conflict_detection_deconfliction_message_content` with handoff-contract assertion.

Validation evidence:
1) `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs` ✅
2) `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-mistydog-3681t-3-7-1 rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core conflict_detection_auto_resolved_messages_include_handoff_roles -- --nocapture`
   - rch attempted remote offload but failed open to local due rsync churn.
   - build blocked by pre-existing unrelated compile errors:
     - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/command_handoff.rs:131:23` unresolved crate/module `shlex` (E0433)
     - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/command_handoff.rs:140:8` type inference error (E0282)
3) `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-mistydog-3681t-3-7-1 rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib`
   - remote sync fallback to local; run then failed in external toolchain path while compiling vendored openssl (`openssl-sys` build script header expansion / missing `build/expando.c` after local fallback environment churn).

Findings:
- Reproduced a distinct local-side root cause not covered by the earlier remote-failure-only guard: when local TMPDIR is a long /var/folders/... path on macOS, rch's SSH ControlMaster socket path exceeds the Unix socket limit.
- Symptom: workers probe / remote exec can fail even though manual SSH works; rch then risks fail-open local execution.
- Confirmed workaround: prefix local rch invocations with TMPDIR=/tmp. Setting TMPDIR only inside the remote command is insufficient because the failure happens in the local rch SSH wrapper.

Changes:
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh now routes every rch invocation through a Darwin-aware wrapper that applies TMPDIR=/tmp locally, logs that workaround, and broadens fail-open detection to include the socket-path-too-long signature.
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test now covers env-prefixed rch commands (TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- ...) so policy classification continues to treat them as compliant rch usage.

- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh ✅

- Failure signature on `vmi1227854`:
  `failed to read /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`

Interpretation:
- This looks like worker-topology drift / inconsistent sibling-repo provisioning across the fleet: some workers (at least `vmi1149989`) have the required `frankenredis` sibling tree, while others (`vmi1227854`) do not.
- This is distinct from the macOS TMPDIR/SSH-control-socket workaround: even with `TMPDIR=/tmp`, remote execution can still fail if the selected worker lacks the expected sibling repo layout.

Potential follow-up:
- audit which workers have `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/...` populated
- make worker topology consistent or teach `rch` selection to avoid workers missing required sibling repos for this project
- revisit `.rch/config.toml` exclusions and remote path expectations so path dependencies are not silently worker-dependent.
